notes: Front-page 'Rioting of Operatives at Mills in Lawrence': militia, bayonets, ~30,000 idle. A distant Western paper that frames the strike almost entirely as riot/disorder; no improvement response reported.
Front-page 'THE LAWRENCE STRIKE'; 'Sunday passes very quietly' + strikers' committees toward ending strike + call for investigation + 'better wages'. Both order-restoration and improvement signals.
Continued page-one/inside coverage; sustained strike pressure with mixed framing.
Capital paper: 'Immediate Congressional Interference in the Lawrence strike', resolution for congressional investigation, House Rules Committee hearings. Institutional/improvement lens dominant.
Front-page 'LAWRENCE STRIKERS SHOOT FROM AMBUSH ... Disorder Reigns' AND 'pending a congressional investigation'. Disorder and institutional pathways appear in the same issue.
'INVESTIGATION BY CONGRESS'; House committee on rules hearing Saturday on the Lawrence strike. Improvement/institutional response prominent.
'INVESTIGATION TO COME': Senate/House committee examines conditions, child-labor and wage testimony. Improvement pathway, with residual disorder references.
Strike committee votes to declare strike off 'because of the wage increases granted'; Haywood: 'greatest victory ever won by labor'. Strong improvement/settlement framing.
